scope: AgenC memory extraction trigger decisions live in runtime/src/memory/extraction-triggers.ts and are consumed by the extraction service before it launches child agents.

requiredBehaviors: Extraction only counts model-visible user and assistant messages.
If compaction shrinks visible history below the processed cursor, the trigger reconsiders retained visible messages instead of permanently disabling extraction.
edgeCases: Hidden system messages are excluded from trigger ranges.

requiredBehaviors: Successful main-agent writes to absolute memory paths skip child extraction and advance the cursor.
Failed writes, untracked writes, or relative writes do not skip extraction.
edgeCases: Malformed tool arguments are treated as empty objects.
Only completed non-error tool results count as direct memory writes.

requiredBehaviors: Extraction triggers only from the main agent context.
The AGENC_DISABLE_EXTRACT_MEMORIES env flag disables extraction.
Minimum eligible-turn cadence throttles normal extraction but never defers trailing coalesced runs.
edgeCases: Nested or subagent turn contexts do not trigger memory extraction.

requiredBehaviors: The extraction service consumes memory-owned trigger primitives before scanning memories and building the extraction prompt.
Commit-phase scheduling still launches extraction after natural terminal turns and skips pending-tool turns.
edgeCases: Child policy denials and failed child writes keep the cursor eligible for retry.

requiredBehaviors: The legacy query stop-hook path no longer calls memory extraction with the donor-era hook context shape.
Memory extraction scheduling remains owned by the commit phase, where a real Session, TurnContext, and completed tool results are available.
edgeCases: The @ts-nocheck stop-hook boundary cannot hide a stale executeExtractMemories call.
